stem: Data set A: $7, 11, 15, 19$. Calculate the mean of data set A.
hint: Add all the values and divide by the number of values.

stem: Data set B: $5, 10, 14, 19$. Calculate the mean of data set B.
hint: Add all the values and divide by the number of values.

stem: Data set A: $7, 11, 15, 19$ has a mean of $13$. Data set B: $5, 10, 14, 19$ has a mean of $12$. Which statement is correct?
options: Data set A has a higher mean.
Data set B has a higher mean.
Both data sets have the same mean.
The means cannot be compared because the data sets are different.
answer: Data set A has a higher mean.
hint: Compare the two mean values directly.
explanation: Data set A has a mean of $13$ and data set B has a mean of $12$. Since $13 > 12$, data set A has the higher mean.

stem: Data set A: $15, 18, 21, 24, 27$. Data set B: $14, 19, 22, 25, 30$. By how much is data set B's mean greater than data set A's mean?
hint: Calculate the mean of each data set, then find the difference.

stem: Data set P (sorted): $8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32$. Data set Q (sorted): $5, 11, 17, 20, 23, 29, 37$. Calculate the range of data set P.
Data set P (sorted): $8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32$. Data set Q (sorted): $5, 11, 17, 20, 23, 29, 37$. Calculate the range of data set Q.
Data set P (sorted): $8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32$. Calculate the IQR of data set P.
hint: For 7 values, the median is the 4th value. Q1 is the median of the lower three values and Q3 is the median of the upper three values.
explanation: The median of the 7 values is the 4th value: $20$. The lower half is $8, 12, 16$, so $\text{Q1} = 12$. The upper half is $24, 28, 32$, so $\text{Q3} = 28$. $$\text{IQR P} = 28 - 12 = 16$$

stem: Data set Q (sorted): $5, 11, 17, 20, 23, 29, 37$. Calculate the IQR of data set Q.
hint: For 7 values, the median is the 4th value. Find the median of each half to get Q1 and Q3.
explanation: The median of the 7 values is the 4th value: $20$. The lower half is $5, 11, 17$, so $\text{Q1} = 11$. The upper half is $23, 29, 37$, so $\text{Q3} = 29$. $$\text{IQR Q} = 29 - 11 = 18$$

stem: Factory A has a median daily output of $145$ units with an IQR of $18$. Factory B has a median daily output of $138$ units with an IQR of $12$. Which statement correctly identifies the more consistent factory?
options: Factory A is more consistent because it has a higher median.
Factory A is more consistent because it has a larger IQR.
Factory B is more consistent because it has a smaller IQR.
Factory B is more consistent because it has a lower median.
answer: Factory B is more consistent because it has a smaller IQR.
hint: A smaller IQR means the middle 50% of values are closer together, indicating more consistent data.
explanation: Consistency is measured by spread, not by the size of the typical value. Factory A has IQR $= 18$ and Factory B has IQR $= 12$. Since $12 < 18$, Factory B has less variability in its output and is therefore more consistent, even though its median is lower.

stem: Data set X (sorted): $28, 32, 36, 40, 44, 48, 52$. Data set Y (sorted): $20, 30, 36, 40, 44, 50, 62$. Calculate the IQR of data set X.
hint: The median of 7 values is the 4th value. Find Q1 and Q3 from the lower and upper halves.
explanation: The median is the 4th value: $40$. The lower half is $28, 32, 36$, so $\text{Q1} = 32$. The upper half is $44, 48, 52$, so $\text{Q3} = 48$. $$\text{IQR X} = 48 - 32 = 16$$

stem: Data set X (sorted): $28, 32, 36, 40, 44, 48, 52$. Data set Y (sorted): $20, 30, 36, 40, 44, 50, 62$. Calculate the IQR of data set Y.
hint: The median of 7 values is the 4th value. Find Q1 and Q3 from the lower and upper halves.
explanation: The median is the 4th value: $40$. The lower half is $20, 30, 36$, so $\text{Q1} = 30$. The upper half is $44, 50, 62$, so $\text{Q3} = 50$. $$\text{IQR Y} = 50 - 30 = 20$$

stem: Data set X has a median of $40$ and an IQR of $16$. Data set Y has a median of $40$ and an IQR of $20$. Which statement best compares the two data sets?
options: Data set X is more consistent because it has a smaller IQR, while both data sets have the same median.
Data set Y is more consistent because it has a larger IQR, while both data sets have the same median.
Data set X has a higher centre, so it is more consistent.
The data sets cannot be compared because they have the same median.
answer: Data set X is more consistent because it has a smaller IQR, while both data sets have the same median.
hint: When medians are equal, use the IQR to determine which data set has less spread.
explanation: Both data sets have the same median ($40$), so there is no difference in the typical value. Data set X has IQR $= 16$ and data set Y has IQR $= 20$. Since $16 < 20$, data set X has less spread. Its values are more tightly clustered around the median, so data set X is more consistent.
